description: Introduce a pluggable framework pack architecture supporting PASTA, 4QF+STRIDE, LINDDUN Pro, and MAESTRO threat modeling methodologies via a --framework CLI flag.

userStories.title: Framework pack data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a framework pack data model that defines the structure of a threat modeling methodology so that the orchestrator can run any framework without methodology-specific code.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/__init__.py` exports `FrameworkPack`, `StageSpec`, `StageContext`, and `get_framework(name)` function
`StageSpec` dataclass has fields: `number`, `name`, `output_file`, `build_prompt` (callable)

`FrameworkPack` dataclass has fields: `name`, `display_name`, `description`, `stages` (list of StageSpec), `report_stage` (StageSpec), `scanner_stages` (list of int), `reference_sets` (dict mapping stage number to reference config)
`StageContext` dataclass has fields: `user_objectives` (dict | None), `prior_outputs` (dict), `scanners_available` (list[str] | None), `references` (list[str])
`get_framework` returns the correct pack for `stride-4q`, `pasta`, `linddun`, `maestro`
`get_framework` raises `ValueError` for unknown framework names with a message listing available frameworks
Tests cover: data model construction, get_framework for all valid names, get_framework for invalid name
Tests pass
userStories.notes: FrameworkPack, StageSpec, and StageContext dataclasses in frameworks/types.py; registry with register_framework/get_framework; __init__.py re-exports public API including StageContext

userStories.title: Framework registry and discovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a framework registry so that available frameworks are discoverable at runtime for CLI help and validation.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/__init__.py` exports `list_frameworks() -> list[FrameworkPack]` function
`list_frameworks()` returns all four registered framework packs
Each pack in the list has a valid `name`, `display_name`, and `description`
Framework registration requires no code changes to the registry module itself — each framework's pack builder registers via the pack builder pattern
Tests cover: list_frameworks returns all four frameworks, each has required fields
Tests pass
userStories.notes: list_frameworks() in types.py; _built_in.py registers all four placeholder packs at import time; __init__.py imports _built_in to trigger registration

userStories.title: Directory restructuring for framework packs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want framework code organized under `frameworks/` so that each framework's prompts, pack builder, and references are co-located and independently maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/references/` directory exists with `__init__.py` and shared reference modules: `owasp.py`, `scanner_snippets.py`, `conditions.py`
`src/threatsmith/frameworks/stride_4q/` directory exists with `__init__.py`
`src/threatsmith/frameworks/pasta/` directory exists with `__init__.py` and all PASTA stage modules
`src/threatsmith/frameworks/linddun/` directory exists with `__init__.py`
`src/threatsmith/frameworks/maestro/` directory exists with `__init__.py`
`src/threatsmith/prompts/` package is deleted entirely — no backward-compat layer
OWASP and scanner snippet constants are in `frameworks/references/owasp.py` and `frameworks/references/scanner_snippets.py`
`evaluate_reference_conditions()` is in `frameworks/references/conditions.py`, re-exported from `frameworks/references/__init__.py`
Assembler is at `src/threatsmith/assembler.py` (top-level module, not under frameworks/)
All existing import paths are updated so all existing tests pass after relocation
No import anywhere references `threatsmith.prompts`
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Restructured: prompts/ deleted entirely; all framework code under frameworks/; references under frameworks/references/; assembler at top-level src/threatsmith/assembler.py; no circular imports

userStories.title: Conditional reference injection utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable utility for conditional reference injection so that all frameworks can use keyword-based detection for OWASP and other reference sets.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/references/__init__.py` exports `evaluate_reference_conditions(reference_config: list[dict], prior_outputs: dict) -> list[str]` function
`always` condition returns the reference string unconditionally
`api_detected` condition matches case-insensitively against keywords: REST, GraphQL, gRPC, API gateway, endpoint, OpenAPI, Swagger
`llm_detected` condition matches case-insensitively against keywords: LangChain, OpenAI, LLM, vector database, embedding, model serving, prompt, agent framework, Claude, GPT
Returns a list of reference constant values to inject
Keyword lists are configurable constants (not hardcoded in the function body)

Tests cover: always condition, api_detected with matching text, api_detected with non-matching text, llm_detected with matching text, llm_detected with non-matching text, multiple conditions in one call
Tests pass
userStories.notes: evaluate_reference_conditions() in frameworks/references/conditions.py, re-exported from frameworks/references/__init__.py; supports always/api_detected/llm_detected conditions; keyword lists as module-level constants API_KEYWORDS and LLM_KEYWORDS

userStories.title: STRIDE category reference constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STRIDE category definitions as a constant so that the 4QF+STRIDE framework can inject them as a systematic reference.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/references/stride_categories.py` exports `STRIDE_CATEGORIES` string constant
Contains all six STRIDE categories with one-line descriptions: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege
Formatted as a concise reference block suitable for prompt injection
Tests verify all six categories are present by initial letter (S, T, R, I, D, E)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: STRIDE_CATEGORIES constant in frameworks/references/stride_categories.py; concise markdown table with bold full category names; 7 tests in test_stride_categories.py

userStories.title: LINDDUN threat catalogue reference constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LINDDUN threat type definitions as a constant so that the LINDDUN Pro framework can inject them as a systematic reference.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/references/linddun_catalogue.py` exports `LINDDUN_THREAT_TYPES` string constant with all seven categories: Linking, Identifying, Non-repudiation, Detecting, Data Disclosure, Unawareness, Non-compliance
Each category includes the privacy property it protects
Module exports `LINDDUN_DFD_PATTERNS` string constant mapping DFD element types (process, data store, data flow, external entity) to common threat patterns per LINDDUN category
Tests verify all seven categories are present in `LINDDUN_THREAT_TYPES`
Tests verify all four DFD element types are present in `LINDDUN_DFD_PATTERNS`
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F03 (references package must exist under frameworks/).

userStories.title: MITRE ATLAS reference constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MITRE ATLAS technique references as a constant so that the MAESTRO framework can inject them as a systematic reference.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/references/mitre_atlas.py` exports `MITRE_ATLAS_TECHNIQUES` string constant
Contains ATLAS tactics with high-relevance techniques, each with ID and one-line description
Covers at minimum: Reconnaissance, Resource Development, Initial Access, ML Attack Staging, ML Model Access, Exfiltration, Impact
Formatted as a concise reference block suitable for prompt injection
Module documents the ATLAS version targeted in a comment
Tests verify all seven tactic categories are present
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F03 (references package must exist under frameworks/).

userStories.title: PASTA migration into pack structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the existing PASTA prompts migrated into the framework pack structure with zero behavioral changes so that PASTA continues to work identically.
acceptanceCriteria: All PASTA stage prompt modules are at `src/threatsmith/frameworks/pasta/` (stage_01 through stage_08)
`src/threatsmith/frameworks/pasta/__init__.py` exports `build_pasta_pack() -> FrameworkPack`
Pack builder implementation is in `src/threatsmith/frameworks/pasta/_pack.py`
The returned pack defines 7 analysis stages + 1 report stage with correct output filenames matching original `_STAGE_FILES` mapping
`scanner_stages` is `[5]`
`reference_sets` on stage 4 includes OWASP Web Top 10 with `always` condition, OWASP API Top 10 with `api_detected` condition, OWASP LLM Top 10 with `llm_detected` condition
All PASTA `build_prompt` functions use the generic `StageContext` dataclass (not per-stage typed contexts)
OWASP keyword detection is handled by the assembler via `evaluate_reference_conditions()`, not by stage_04's `build_prompt`
Stage 4's `build_prompt` receives pre-resolved references via `context.references`
All existing PASTA tests pass with updated import paths (`frameworks.pasta` not `prompts.pasta`)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: All 8 stage modules under frameworks/pasta/; build_pasta_pack() in _pack.py; all build_prompt functions use StageContext; OWASP injection centralized in assembler; no circular imports (pack builder imports from frameworks.types directly)

userStories.title: Orchestrator refactor for framework-agnostic execution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the orchestrator to iterate over a framework pack's stage list instead of hardcoded stage numbers so that it can run any framework.
acceptanceCriteria: `Orchestrator.__init__` accepts a `pack: FrameworkPack` parameter replacing any hardcoded stage assumptions
`Orchestrator.run()` iterates `pack.stages` then runs `pack.report_stage`
Output validation checks for `stage.output_file` instead of hardcoded filenames
Context accumulation uses `stage.output_file` as the key for reading deliverables into `_prior_outputs`
The orchestrator does not import or reference any framework-specific modules
Tests cover: orchestrator with a mock 3-stage pack, orchestrator with PASTA pack (regression), output validation with framework-defined filenames
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation was complete from Deliverable A restructure. Added missing tests: mock 3-stage pack (test_mock_3stage_pack_full_pipeline_success), output validation with framework filenames (test_mock_pack_output_validation_uses_framework_filenames), context accumulation with non-PASTA pack (test_mock_pack_context_accumulates).

userStories.title: Assembler refactor for framework-agnostic prompt assembly
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the assembler to use the framework pack's stage definitions and reference configuration instead of hardcoded stage-to-module mappings.
acceptanceCriteria: Assembler is at `src/threatsmith/assembler.py` (top-level module)
`assemble_prompt` accepts a `StageSpec` and `FrameworkPack` instead of a stage number
`assemble_prompt` builds a `StageContext` and calls `stage.build_prompt(context, output_dir=output_dir)` directly
Scanner snippets are injected only when the stage number is in `pack.scanner_stages`
Reference sets are resolved from `pack.reference_sets[stage.number]` using `evaluate_reference_conditions`
All existing assembler tests are updated to use the new interface
Tests cover: assembly with scanner-eligible stage, assembly with non-scanner stage, assembly with conditional references (always, api_detected, llm_detected)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation was complete from Deliverable A restructure. Added TestFrameworkAgnosticAssembly class with 5 tests using a custom non-PASTA pack to explicitly verify scanner injection, non-scanner exclusion, and all three reference conditions (always, api_detected, llm_detected).

userStories.title: 4QF+STRIDE Stage 1 prompt — System Model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for the 4QF+STRIDE System Model stage so that the agent produces a comprehensive application model as the foundation for threat identification.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/stride_4q/stage_01_system_model.py` exports `STAGE_PROMPT` string constant
Prompt covers: application purpose and scope, technology stack, data flows, actors and assets, trust boundaries, entry points, external dependencies, deployment context
Prompt instructs the agent to produce at minimum one Mermaid DFD
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to `threatmodel/01-system-model.md`
Module exports `build_prompt(context: StageContext, output_dir: str = "threatmodel") -> str` that injects user objectives when provided and omits the objectives section when not provided
Tests cover: build_prompt with objectives, build_prompt without objectives
Tests pass

userStories.notes: Depends on US-F03 (maestro package under frameworks/).

userStories.title: MAESTRO Stage 5 prompt — Attack Modeling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for the MAESTRO Attack Modeling stage with AI-specific attack trees and MITRE ATLAS mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/maestro/stage_05_attack_modeling.py` exports `STAGE_PROMPT` string constant

Prompt covers: Mermaid attack trees for high-priority AI threats, multi-stage attack paths chaining AI and traditional vulnerabilities, MITRE ATLAS technique mapping with tactic/technique IDs, feasibility assessment (attacker skill, access, tooling, cost), impact assessment
Prompt instructs particular focus on attack paths traversing AI and non-AI components
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to `threatmodel/05-attack-modeling.md`
Module exports `build_prompt(context: StageContext, output_dir: str = "threatmodel") -> str` that injects Stages 1–4 outputs and MITRE ATLAS reference via `context.references`
Tests cover: build_prompt with prior context and MITRE ATLAS reference appears in output
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F07 (MITRE ATLAS constants), US-F03 (maestro package under frameworks/).

userStories.title: MAESTRO Stage 6 prompt — Risk and Mitigation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for the MAESTRO Risk and Mitigation stage with AI-specific countermeasures and remediation roadmap.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/maestro/stage_06_risk_mitigation.py` exports `STAGE_PROMPT` string constant

Prompt covers: risk qualification per attack path, AI-specific countermeasures (input/output guardrails, prompt hardening, model access controls, training pipeline integrity, agent sandboxing, tool authorization, output content filtering, model monitoring and anomaly detection), traditional countermeasures in AI context, P0–P3 remediation roadmap with effort and risk reduction, residual risk assessment
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to `threatmodel/06-risk-mitigation.md`
Module exports `build_prompt(context: StageContext, output_dir: str = "threatmodel") -> str` that injects Stages 1–5 outputs
Tests cover: build_prompt with prior stage context injected
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F03 (maestro package under frameworks/).

userStories.title: MAESTRO Stage 7 prompt — Report Consolidation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a prompt template for the MAESTRO report consolidation step.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/maestro/stage_07_report.py` exports `STAGE_PROMPT` string constant
Prompt instructs consolidation of Stages 1–6 into a single AI security risk report with no new analysis
Prompt instructs preservation of architecture diagrams, attack trees, MITRE ATLAS mappings, and remediation roadmap
Prompt instructs the agent to write output to `threatmodel/07-report.md`
Module exports `build_prompt(context: StageContext, output_dir: str = "threatmodel") -> str` that injects Stages 1–6 outputs
Tests cover: build_prompt with all six stage outputs injected
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F03 (maestro package under frameworks/).

userStories.title: MAESTRO pack builder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pack builder function that assembles the MAESTRO framework pack.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/threatsmith/frameworks/maestro/__init__.py` exports `build_maestro_pack() -> FrameworkPack`
Pack has `name="maestro"` and `display_name="MAESTRO"`
Pack defines exactly 6 analysis stages and 1 report stage with correct output filenames and `build_prompt` references
`scanner_stages` is `[4]`
`reference_sets` includes `MITRE_ATLAS_TECHNIQUES` with `always` and `OWASP_LLM_TOP_10` with `always` on stage 3, and `MITRE_ATLAS_TECHNIQUES` with `always` on stage 5
Tests verify pack `name`, `display_name`, stage count (6 + report), `scanner_stages`, and reference_sets configuration on stages 3 and 5
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F01 (FrameworkPack), US-F07 (MITRE ATLAS), US-F24 through US-F30 (all MAESTRO stage modules).

userStories.title: CLI --framework flag and --list-frameworks
userStories.description: As a user, I want a `--framework` flag to select the threat modeling methodology and a `--list-frameworks` command to see available options.
acceptanceCriteria: `main.py` adds `--framework` option with default `stride-4q` and choices validated against the framework registry
`main.py` adds `--list-frameworks` flag that prints available frameworks with names and descriptions then exits with code 0
Selected framework pack is passed to the orchestrator
`--framework` value is recorded in `metadata.json`
`--rerun-stage` validates stage numbers against the selected framework's stage count and prints an informative error (including valid range) for out-of-range values
Config file `framework` field is respected, with CLI `--framework` taking precedence when both are specified

Tests cover: default framework is `stride-4q`, explicit `--framework pasta` selection, `--list-frameworks` output contains all four framework names, invalid framework name produces error, `--rerun-stage 7` with `stride-4q` produces error
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F01, US-F02 (registry), US-F09 (orchestrator accepts framework), US-F08 (PASTA pack), US-F16 (STRIDE pack), US-F23 (LINDDUN pack), US-F31 (MAESTRO pack).

userStories.title: Metadata framework tracking
userStories.description: As a user, I want `metadata.json` to record which framework was used so that re-runs, updates, and tooling can identify the methodology.
acceptanceCriteria: `generate_metadata` accepts a `framework: FrameworkPack` parameter
`metadata.json` includes `framework` field (pack `name`) and `framework_display_name` field (pack `display_name`)
`metadata.json` includes `stages_completed` count
When resuming or re-running, the wrapper logs a warning if the current `--framework` differs from the `framework` value recorded in existing metadata
Tests cover: metadata JSON contains `framework` and `framework_display_name` fields with correct values, framework mismatch warning is logged at WARNING level
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F01 (FrameworkPack), US-F32 (CLI provides framework to metadata).

userStories.title: End-to-end test for 4QF+STRIDE pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an end-to-end test verifying the full 4QF+STRIDE pipeline from CLI invocation to deliverable output.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/test_e2e_stride_4q.py` tests the full pipeline with `--framework stride-4q` using a mock engine
Test verifies all 5 deliverable files are created: `01-system-model.md`, `02-threat-identification.md`, `03-mitigations.md`, `04-validation.md`, `05-report.md`
Test verifies Stage 2 prompt contains STRIDE categories reference
Test verifies metadata includes `framework: "stride-4q"`
Test verifies context accumulates across all 4 analysis stages (each stage's prompt includes prior stage outputs)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F16 (STRIDE pack), US-F32 (CLI), US-F33 (metadata). E2E test — must come after all component stories.

userStories.title: End-to-end test for LINDDUN Pro pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an end-to-end test verifying the full LINDDUN Pro pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/test_e2e_linddun.py` tests the full pipeline with `--framework linddun` using a mock engine
Test verifies all 6 deliverable files are created: `01-system-context.md`, `02-data-flow.md`, `03-threat-elicitation.md`, `04-threat-prioritization.md`, `05-mitigation.md`, `06-report.md`
Test verifies Stage 3 prompt contains LINDDUN catalogue reference
Test verifies no scanner context is injected into any stage (scanner_stages is empty)
Test verifies metadata includes `framework: "linddun"`
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F23 (LINDDUN pack), US-F32 (CLI), US-F33 (metadata).

userStories.title: End-to-end test for MAESTRO pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an end-to-end test verifying the full MAESTRO pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/test_e2e_maestro.py` tests the full pipeline with `--framework maestro` using a mock engine

Test verifies all 7 deliverable files are created: `01-ai-system-profiling.md`, `02-architecture.md`, `03-threat-identification.md`, `04-vulnerability-analysis.md`, `05-attack-modeling.md`, `06-risk-mitigation.md`, `07-report.md`
Test verifies Stage 3 prompt contains MITRE ATLAS reference and OWASP LLM Top 10
Test verifies Stage 4 prompt contains scanner context when scanners are available
Test verifies metadata includes `framework: "maestro"`
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F31 (MAESTRO pack), US-F32 (CLI), US-F33 (metadata).

userStories.title: PASTA regression test after migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a regression test confirming that PASTA behaves identically after migration into the pack structure.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/test_e2e_pasta_regression.py` runs the full PASTA pipeline with `--framework pasta` using a mock engine
Test verifies all 8 deliverable files are created with original filenames: `01-objectives.md` through `07-risk-and-impact-analysis.md` and `08-report.md`
Test verifies Stage 4 prompt contains OWASP Web Top 10 always, OWASP API Top 10 when API keywords present, OWASP LLM Top 10 when LLM keywords present (same conditional logic as pre-refactor)
Test verifies Stage 5 prompt contains scanner snippets (same injection logic as pre-refactor)
Test verifies metadata includes `framework: "pasta"`
Test assertions correspond to existing `test_e2e.py` assertions (adapted for the `--framework` flag)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F08 (PASTA pack), US-F32 (CLI), US-F33 (metadata). Regression gate — must run after all PASTA migration stories.
